summary: End-to-end testing of new catalog features: PUT/duplicate/share/unshare/public endpoints (backend) + full frontend flows (edit, duplicate, share dialog, public viewer without auth, disable share). All 9 pytest cases + all 8 Playwright scenarios passed.
critical_code_review_comments: CatalogCreate.jsx continues to grow (~700+ lines) now with edit-mode branch via useParams; consider extracting the edit-loading effect + save handler into a custom hook.

PUT /catalogs/{id} accepts any user-supplied fields directly via CatalogUpdate model; verify Pydantic model whitelists only name/settings/product_ids to prevent mass-assignment (e.g., user_id, share_token being overwritten by client).
Public file endpoint /api/files/{path}?share={token} performs a linear scan of catalog product_ids on every request; acceptable for now but could be indexed if scale grows.
Share token uses secrets.token_urlsafe(16) (~128 bits) — good, unguessable.
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_catalog_share.py (new pytest module for the 6 new endpoints)
seed_data_creation: Attached uploaded PNG (69 bytes) to product Prod Bulk 1 (id 6a591361fed065430c05b433) so file share access could be verified. Path: catalog-forge/uploads/6a58fd0c2a6c1ba69ae2747e/1189bafb-7c24-479c-afd6-42ad5e9abbcd.png
context_for_next_testing_agent:
Verified: (1) Sidebar 'Crea nuovo catalogo' link. (2) /app/catalog title 'Crea nuovo catalogo'. (3) /app/catalog/edit/{id} title 'Modifica catalogo' + data-testid='back-to-history' present, pre-loads existing catalog. (4) History cards expose edit/duplicate/share/delete testids per catalog id. (5) Duplicate adds new card at top with '(copia)' suffix. (6) Share dialog: enable-share -> share-link input + copy-share-link + open-share-link visible; public-badge-{id} appears. (7) Disable-share removes badge. (8) PUT /api/catalogs/{id} returns 200 and shows 'Catalogo aggiornato' toast. (9) Public /c/{token} renders
WITHOUT auth in fresh browser context; public-print button present. (10) Backend: PUT/duplicate/share (idempotent)/unshare/public-fetch (404 after unshare)/file access via ?share= all validated including 401 for invalid token and image bytes served without auth. Note: during frontend flow the catalog being shared happened to have 0 products included after the Save step, so no tags appeared on the public page — this is test-data specific, backend image-serving test already covered the byte-level behavior successfully.
